# Example Output: Claude's response using system_prompt.md

*This was generated by pasting `system_prompt.md` as a system prompt, then
providing the bullet points in `example_input.md`. It demonstrates the
define-then-translate pattern, inline data with a concrete comparison,
and point-first structure.*

---

The modern payments system is, when you stop to think about it, a minor miracle.
A set of standards and protocols built largely in the 1970s and 80s now underpins
trillions of dollars in daily global commerce. The same invisible infrastructure
quietly works whether you're buying groceries in Columbus or settling a trade in
Singapore. Decades of accumulated decisions, most of them made before the internet
existed, hold together something the global economy now depends on completely.
Most people never think about it. That's exactly how it's supposed to work.

Meridian is our piece of that infrastructure. It's the platform governing
real-time credit limit decisions across our prepaid and credit card portfolio.
In simple terms: the system deciding in milliseconds whether to approve or decline
a transaction every time a customer uses their card. At $250B in annual transaction
volume, roughly the GDP of Portugal, those decisions are consequential in both
directions. A false decline means a customer standing at a register, card in hand,
wondering why it isn't working. An over-approval means credit exposure we absorb
later. When Meridian works, it's invisible. When it doesn't, the product has failed
to do the one thing it exists to do.

The current system was built in 2017 on static rules. It hasn't materially changed
since interest rates were near zero and our product mix looked nothing like it does
today. This PRD proposes replacing it with a dynamic, ML-driven decisioning engine
that learns from customer behavior rather than encoding assumptions about it.

---

## What changed from v1 and why

| Feedback | Old version | New version | 4D lens |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zoom out first | Opened on Meridian directly | Opens on the history of payments infrastructure | **Delegation** — you provided the insight; AI restructured around it |
| Consequences face outward | "career-defining when it doesn't" (internal) | Customer at the register (external) | **Discernment** — caught that the tone was off, not just the words |
| Verbal vs. written register | "career-defining" works in a room, reads cold in a doc | Cut; replaced with product-purpose framing | **Discernment** — recognized the gap between how it sounded and how it should read |
| Humanity | No customer present in the paragraph | Customer appears in a specific, human situation | **Description** — adding this context to the prompt produced the right output |
| No hedging | ✓ | ✓ still holds | — |
